content.verdict: The Sofitel Bora Bora Private Island sits on its own motu entirely separate from the main island, offering 100 bungalows ranging from beach to overwater options at some of the best value pricing in the Bora Bora luxury tier. It is one of the island's most popular wedding venues, with the infrastructure for romance deeply embedded in how the resort operates — ideal for couples who want the full Bora Bora dream without the stratospheric pricing of the St. Regis or Four Seasons.
content.best_room: The Overwater Bungalow with Private Pool is the standout choice for honeymooners — a private plunge pool over the lagoon, glass floor panels to watch the fish below, and your own sun deck with direct lagoon ladder access. The unobstructed views of Mount Otemanu from these bungalows are among the best on the property.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Motu Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the resort's shuttle boat from Bora Bora's main island to the private motu. Your room will be decorated with flower petals and a welcome champagne awaits. Watch the sun set over Mount Otemanu from your bungalow deck as herons skim the lagoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Overwater & Lagoon Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Swim directly from your bungalow ladder into the clear lagoon — the coral directly below the overwater section supports a dazzling variety of fish. Book a couple's massage at the Manea Spa using monoi and Tahitian noni treatments developed for island relaxation.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lagoon Safari
itinerary_7_nights.description: Join a guided 4x4 lagoon tour by boat — visiting the famous Bora Bora shark and ray feeding area where you can wade among nurse sharks in shallow water. The guide will take you to the most vivid coral patches for snorkeling before returning for a beach barbecue lunch.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Exploration & Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rent kayaks from the resort's watersports center and paddle around the motu at your own pace. Find a quiet stretch of beach for the afternoon — the sofitel's own beach is white sand and calm, ideal for a long, lazy afternoon under a palm umbrella.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunset Cruise
itinerary_7_nights.description: Book the resort's private sunset sailing cruise — a catamaran departure just before golden hour, circumnavigating Bora Bora's iconic silhouette as the sky turns orange and pink. Champagne is served on deck with fresh Tahitian poisson cru.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Private Dining
itinerary_7_nights.description: Dedicate the morning to the resort's full spa menu — a Polynesian wrap, deep tissue massage, and reflexology session before a light lunch by the lagoon pool. This evening, arrange a private overwater dinner on your bungalow deck — the resort's most romantic dining option.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Last Lagoon Swim & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take a final swim in the lagoon at sunrise before a lingering breakfast. The boat transfer back to the main island takes just minutes, but Bora Bora — and the memory of your motu — will stay with you forever.

caveats: As one of Bora Bora's more accessible luxury options, the resort can feel less intimate than smaller properties during peak season when all 100 bungalows are occupied.
The motu location means you are dependent on the shuttle boat for access to Bora Bora's main island — the boats run regularly but last departures are typically by 11pm.
Some overwater bungalows have slightly obstructed lagoon views from neighbours; request end-of-pier positioning when booking for maximum seclusion.

faqs.question: Is the Sofitel Bora Bora good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — it is one of Bora Bora's most established romance destinations and one of the island's top wedding venues. The private island setting, overwater bungalows, and competitive pricing for the tier make it an excellent honeymoon choice.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May through October offers the driest and sunniest conditions with the best lagoon visibility for snorkeling. July and August are peak season; April, May, and September offer excellent conditions with fewer crowds.

faqs.question: How far in advance should you book?
faqs.answer: Book 3-6 months ahead for peak dates. As one of the larger properties on Bora Bora, availability is slightly better than the smaller ultra-luxury resorts, but honeymoon-popular dates fill quickly.

faqs.question: Is the resort adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, but it is primarily a couples resort given its wedding venue status. Families do visit but the overwater bungalow experience keeps the vibe strongly romantic.

faqs.question: What is the best room type?
faqs.answer: The Overwater Bungalow with Private Pool offers the best combination of privacy, romance, and lagoon immersion. For value, the standard Overwater Bungalow delivers the full Bora Bora experience at a lower price point.

faqs.question: How do you get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Bora Bora Airport via Papeete, then take the resort's complimentary shuttle boat from the main island to the private motu — a scenic 5-minute ride across the lagoon.
